title: God’s Christ, who is my righteousness
firstLine: God’s Christ, who is my righteousness,
textAttribution: Text: Nicolaus L. Zinzendorf (1700–1760), John Wesley (1703–1791).
publicDomainBasis: Nicolaus L. Zinzendorf (1700–1760), John Wesley (1703–1791), Ludwig van Beethoven (1770–1827). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
God’s Christ, who is my righteousness,
My beauty is, my glorious dress;
Midst flaming worlds, in this arrayed,
With joy shall I lift up my head.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Lord, I believe Thy precious blood,
Which, at the mercy seat of God,
Forever doth for sinners plead,
For me, e’en for my soul, was shed.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Lord, I believe were sinners more
Than sands upon the ocean shore,
Thou hast for all a ransom paid,
For all a full redemption made.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
Bold can I stand in every way,
For who aught to my charge shall lay?
Fully, by Thee, absolved I am
From sin and fear, from guilt and shame.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
This spotless robe the same appears,
When ruined nature sinks in years;
No age can change its glorious hue,
Its glory is forever new.

sections.label: Verse 6
sections.text:
Thou God of power, Thou God of love,
Let all Thy saints Thy mercy prove;
Our beauty this, our glorious dress,
Jesus the Lord, our Righteousness.
